+{"id":"bd-396j","title":"GetBlockedIssues shows external deps as blocking even when satisfied","description":"GetBlockedIssues (ready.go:385-493) shows external:* refs in the blocked_by list but doesn't check if they're actually satisfied using CheckExternalDep.

This can be confusing - an issue shows as blocked by external:project:capability even if that capability has been shipped (closed issue with provides: label exists).

Options:
1. Call CheckExternalDep for each external ref and filter satisfied ones from blocked_by
2. Add a note in output indicating external deps need lazy resolution
3. Document this is expected behavior (bd blocked shows all deps, bd ready shows resolved state)

Related: GetReadyWork correctly filters by external deps, but GetBlockedIssues doesn't.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286304-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286304-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-396j","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286971-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-66l4","title":"Runtime bonding: bd mol attach","description":"Attach a molecule to an already-running workflow.

COMMAND: bd mol attach <epic-id> <proto> [--after <issue-id>]

BEHAVIOR:
- Resolve running epic and proto
- Spawn proto as new subtree
- Wire to specified attachment point (or epic root)
- Handle in-progress issues: new work doesn't block completed work

USE CASES:
- Discovered need for docs while implementing feature
- Hotfix needs attaching to release workflow
- Additional testing scope identified mid-flight

FLAGS:
- --after ISSUE: Specific attachment point within epic
- --type: sequential (default) or parallel
- --var: Variables for the attached proto

CONSIDERATIONS:
- What if epic is already closed? Error or reopen?
- What if attachment point issue is closed? Attach as ready-to-work?","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:16.920483-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T01:08:43.530597-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T01:08:43.530597-08:00","close_reason":"Merged into bd-o91r: bond command handles all bonding cases polymorphically","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l4","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.435542-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-66l4","depends_on_id":"bd-o91r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.813782-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-687g","title":"Code review: mol squash deletion bypasses tombstone system","description":"The deleteEphemeralChildren function in mol_squash.go uses DeleteIssue directly instead of the proper deletion flow. This bypasses tombstone creation, deletion tracking (deletions.jsonl), and dependency cleanup. Could cause issues with deletion propagation across clones.

Current code uses d.DeleteIssue(ctx, id) but should probably use d.DeleteIssues(ctx, ids, false, true, false) for proper tombstone handling.

Alternative: Document that ephemeral issues intentionally use hard delete since they are transient and should never propagate to other clones anyway.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:57:20.223345-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:17:38.073899-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:17:38.073899-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented ephemeral issue filtering from JSONL export and fixed comments leak in DeleteIssue"}
+{"id":"bd-687v","title":"Consider caching external dep resolution results","description":"Each call to GetReadyWork re-checks all external dependencies by:
1. Querying for external deps in the local database
2. Opening each external project's database
3. Querying for closed issues with provides: labels

For workloads with many external deps or slow external databases, this adds latency on every bd ready call.

Potential optimizations:
- In-memory TTL cache for external dep status (e.g., 60 second TTL)
- Store resolved status in a local cache table with timestamp
- Batch resolution of common project/capability pairs

This is not urgent - current implementation is correct and performant for typical workloads. Only becomes an issue with many external deps across many projects.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:16.360877-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:16.360877-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-687v","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:16.361493-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-68e4","title":"doctor --fix should export when DB has more issues than JSONL","description":"When 'bd doctor' detects a count mismatch (DB has more issues than JSONL), it currently recommends 'bd sync --import-only', which imports JSONL into DB. But JSONL is the source of truth, not the DB.

**Current behavior:**
- Doctor detects: DB has 355 issues, JSONL has 292
- Recommends: 'bd sync --import-only' 
- User runs it: Returns '0 created, 0 updated' (no-op, because JSONL hasn't changed)
- User is stuck

**Root cause:**
The doctor fix is one-directional (JSONL→DB) when it should be bidirectional. If DB has MORE issues, they haven't been exported yet - the fix should be 'bd export' (DB→JSONL), not import.

**Desired fix:**
In fix.DBJSONLSync(), detect which has more data:
- If DB > JSONL: Run 'bd export' to sync JSONL (since DB is the working copy)
- If JSONL > DB: Run 'bd sync --import-only' to import (JSONL is source of truth)
- If equal but timestamps differ: Detect based on file mtime

This makes 'bd doctor --fix' actually fix the problem instead of being a no-op.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T11:17:20.994319182-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:23:24.38523731-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:23:24.38523731-07:00"}

+{"id":"bd-a3sj","title":"RemoveDependency fails on external deps - FK violation in dirty_issues","description":"In dependencies.go:225, RemoveDependency marks BOTH issueID and dependsOnID as dirty. For external refs (e.g., external:project:capability), dependsOnID doesn't exist in the issues table. This causes FK violation since dirty_issues.issue_id has FK constraint to issues.id.

Fix: Check if dependsOnID starts with 'external:' and only mark source issue as dirty, matching the logic in AddDependency (lines 162-170).

 {"id":"bd-abjw","title":"Consider consolidating config.yaml parsing into shared utility","description":"Multiple places parse config.yaml with custom structs:

1. **autoimport.go:148** - `localConfig{SyncBranch}`
2. **main.go:310** - strings.Contains for no-db (fragile, see bd-r6k2)
3. **doctor.go:863** - strings.Contains for no-db (fragile, see bd-r6k2)
4. **internal/config/config.go** - Uses viper (but caches at startup, problematic for tests)

Consider creating a shared utility in `internal/configfile/` or extending the viper config:

```go
// internal/configfile/yaml.go
type YAMLConfig struct {
    SyncBranch string `yaml:"sync-branch"`
    NoDb       bool   `yaml:"no-db"`
    IssuePrefix string `yaml:"issue-prefix"`
    Author     string `yaml:"author"`
}

func LoadYAML(beadsDir string) (*YAMLConfig, error) {
    // Parse config.yaml with proper YAML library
}
```

Benefits:
- Single source of truth for config.yaml structure
- Proper YAML parsing everywhere
- Easier to add new config fields

Trade-off: May add complexity for simple one-off reads.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T02:03:26.067311-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T02:03:26.067311-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-n3v","title":"Error committing to sync branch: failed to create worktree","description":"> bd sync --no-daemon
→ Exporting pending changes to JSONL...
→ Committing changes to sync branch 'beads-sync'...
Error committing to sync branch: failed to create worktree: failed to create worktree parent directory: mkdir /var/home/matt/dev/beads/fix-ci/.git: not a directory","notes":"**Problem Diagnosed**: The `bd sync` command was failing with "mkdir /var/home/matt/dev/beads/fix-ci/.git: not a directory" because it was being executed from the wrong directory.

**Root Cause**: The command was run from `/var/home/matt/dev/beads` (where the `fix-ci` worktree exists) instead of the main repository directory `/var/home/matt/dev/beads/main`. Since `fix-ci` is a git worktree with a `.git` file (not directory), the worktree creation logic failed when trying to create `<current_dir>/.git/beads-worktrees/<branch>`.

**Solution Verified**: Execute `bd sync` from the main repository directory:
```bash
cd main && bd sync --dry-run
```
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-05T15:25:24.514998248-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-05T15:42:32.910166956-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-n4td","title":"Add warning when staleness check errors","description":"## Problem

When ensureDatabaseFresh() calls CheckStaleness() and it errors (corrupted metadata, permission issues, etc.), we silently proceed with potentially stale data.

**Location:** cmd/bd/staleness.go:27-32

**Scenarios:**
- Corrupted metadata table
- Database locked by another process  
- Permission issues reading JSONL file
- Invalid last_import_time format in DB

## Current Code

```go
isStale, err := autoimport.CheckStaleness(ctx, store, dbPath)
if err \!= nil {
    // If we can't determine staleness, allow operation to proceed
    // (better to show potentially stale data than block user)
    return nil
}
```

## Fix

```go
isStale, err := autoimport.CheckStaleness(ctx, store, dbPath)
if err \!= nil {
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: Could not verify database freshness: %v\n", err)
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Proceeding anyway. Data may be stale.\n\n")
    return nil
}
```

## Impact
Medium - users should know when staleness check fails

## Effort
Easy - 5 minutes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-20T20:16:34.889997-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.531031-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T19:11:12.950618-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n4td","depends_on_id":"bd-2q6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-20T20:18:20.154723-05:00","created_by":"stevey","metadata":"{}"}]}
@@ -384,6 +388,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-uwkp","title":"Phase 2.4: Git merge driver optimization for TOON format","description":"Optimize git 3-way merge for TOON line-oriented format.

## Overview
TOON is line-oriented (unlike binary formats), enabling smarter git merge strategies. Implement custom merge driver to handle TOON-specific merge patterns.

## Required Work

### 2.4.1 TOON Merge Driver
- [ ] Create .git/info/attributes entry for *.toon files
- [ ] Implement custom merge driver script/command
- [ ] Handle tabular format row merges (line-based 3-way)
- [ ] Handle YAML-style format merges
- [ ] Conflict markers for unsolvable conflicts

### 2.4.2 Merge Patterns
- [ ] Row addition: both branches add different rows → union
- [ ] Row deletion: one branch deletes, other modifies → conflict (manual review)
- [ ] Row modification: concurrent field changes → intelligent merge or conflict
- [ ] Field ordering changes: ignore (TOON format resilient to order)

### 2.4.3 Testing & Documentation
- [ ] Unit tests for merge scenarios (3-way merge logic)
- [ ] Integration tests with actual git merges
- [ ] Conflict scenario testing
- [ ] Documentation of merge strategy

## Success Criteria
- Git merge handles TOON conflicts intelligently
- Fewer manual merge conflicts than JSONL
- Round-trip preserved through merges
- All 70+ tests still passing
- Git history stays clean (minimal conflict markers)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:14.339238776-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:26.434306-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:26.434306-08:00","close_reason":"TOON approach declined","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uwkp","depends_on_id":"bd-iic1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:14.34427988-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-uz8r","title":"Phase 2.3: TOON deletion tracking","description":"Implement deletion tracking in TOON format.

## Overview
Phase 2.2 switched storage to TOON format. Phase 2.3 adds deletion tracking in TOON format for propagating deletions across clones.

## Required Work

### 2.3.1 Deletion Tracking (TOON Format)
- [ ] Implement deletions.toon file (tracking deleted issue records)
- [ ] Add DeleteTracker struct to record deleted issue IDs and metadata
- [ ] Update bdt delete command to record in deletions.toon
- [ ] Design deletion record format (ID, timestamp, reason, hash)
- [ ] Implement auto-prune of old deletion records (configurable TTL)

### 2.3.2 Sync Propagation
- [ ] Load deletions.toon during import
- [ ] Remove deleted issues from local database when imported from remote
- [ ] Handle edge cases (delete same issue in multiple clones)
- [ ] Deletion ordering and conflict resolution

### 2.3.3 Testing
- [ ] Unit tests for deletion tracking
- [ ] Integration tests for deletion propagation
- [ ] Multi-clone deletion scenarios
- [ ] TTL expiration tests

## Success Criteria
- deletions.toon stores deletion records in TOON format
- Deletions propagate across clones via git sync
- Old records auto-prune after TTL

+{"id":"bd-vks2","title":"bd dep tree doesn't display external dependencies","description":"GetDependencyTree (dependencies.go:464-624) uses a recursive CTE that JOINs with the issues table, which means external refs (external:project:capability) are invisible in the tree output.

When an issue has an external blocking dependency, running 'bd dep tree <id>' won't show it.

Options:
1. Query dependencies table separately for external refs and display them as leaf nodes
2. Add a synthetic 'external' node type that shows the ref and resolution status
3. Document that external deps aren't shown in tree view (use bd show for full deps)

Lower priority since bd show <id> displays all dependencies including external refs.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:27.121934-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:27.121934-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vks2","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:27.122511-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
